title: cuscuta tetrandra
intro: Cuscuta tetrandra, also known as Dodder vine, is a type of parasitic plant that grows by attaching itself to other plants using its twining stems and leaves. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Cuscuta tetrandra include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain and swelling, and treat various infections, such as the common cold and flu. In herbal preparations, Cuscuta tetrandra is often combined with other herbs to create teas, capsules, and tinctures that can be used to support immune function and overall health.
